description: Canonical KnitBit character-builder manifest. Single source of truth for the base rig, every trait's source mesh + fit transform, and the named preset loadouts. Read by tools/knitbit_manifest.py (pure Python), tools/build_character.py (Blender assembler), and tools/fit_traits.py. See docs/KnitBit-Base-Spec.md sections 4-6.

base.comment: char_height / half_width come from the base mesh bounds printed by tools/rig_knitbit.py (min=(-0.738,-0.371,-0.957) max=(0.733,0.373,0.954), Blender Z-up). All fit.scale values are fractions of char_height; fit.offset_frac is in socket-local space, also as fractions of char_height.

antenna.label: Antenna
antenna.comment: mount:antenna_pair traits fit on socket_head_left_antenna + socket_head_right_antenna (right mirrored on x).

helmet_panel.label: Head Top Panel
helmet_panel.comment: Low-profile surface detail on socket_head_top_center only. Never dome/helmet geometry (the head IS the helmet, spec section 1).

belt_charm.label: Belt Charm
belt_charm.comment: All belt charms share a fit.offset_frac that hangs them off the belt on the character's RIGHT hip (toward socket_belt_right at x=-0.234, ~87% of the way; character's right = -x), with the hook clipped onto the belt band and the body dangling FREELY below, clear of the thigh. The offset: -x toward the right hip (away from center-front), -y just proud enough to clear the thigh's forward bulge (leg front ~-0.154 below the belt), +z so the hanger ring clips onto the belt band's edge rather than floating below it or riding above it. Identical across charms so the placement reads consistently.

dynamic.comment: Belt charms are a DYNAMIC (free-hanging) attachment: the runtime should swing them as a pendulum about the hook when the character moves. build_character.py sets the charm node's origin to the hook (top-center) and writes these params to the node's glTF extras (knitbit_* keys) so the engine's spring/verlet system (see game/lib/physics/verlet_rope.dart) can drive it. Static preview renders show the rest pose; the swing itself is runtime.

accessory.label: Accessory
accessory.comment: Each accessory carries its own socket (backpack -> back_center, headphones -> head_top_center, watering can -> right_hand_grip).

themes._comment: The spec-section-7 theme legend as data. Neutral base = charcoal yarn; each theme is produced later by a yarn material-swap to `accent` plus theme-specific accessories. `accent` doubles as the label/generation reference color and drives the Master Trait Diagram swatches. Each trait carries a `theme` tag keyed to these.

traits.label: Scout Camera
traits.note: 20-deg outward (Y) lean + 0.13 scale keep the camera modules off the base's baked-in antenna nubs and out of the variant_b headphone band. The lean is symmetric under the mirrored -x scale; an X tilt is not.

traits.label: Leaf Tip
traits.note: 0.13 scale so the leaf tips clear the base's baked-in antenna nubs.

traits.label: Reinforced
traits.note: Small -0.01 z offset sinks the plate edges into the curved crown instead of hovering.

traits.label: Leaf
traits.note: This badge reconstructed lying flat (emblem up) unlike the standing wrench badge, so it needs a real +90 X pitch to face the emblem front (verified against -90/180).

traits.label: Gear
traits.note: offset_frac hangs the charm on the front-right of the belt toward the right hip (see the belt_charm slot comment).

traits.label: Coin
traits.note: offset_frac hangs the charm on the front-right of the belt toward the right hip (see the belt_charm slot comment).

traits.label: Flower
traits.note: offset_frac hangs the charm on the front-right of the belt toward the right hip (see the belt_charm slot comment).

traits.label: Headphones
traits.note: 0.62 scale + higher mount (-0.11 z) so the band arcs above and around the antennae instead of slicing through them. Single band unit spanning both ear cups; an arched topper is low-profile, not dome geometry (spec section 1).

traits.label: Watering Can
traits.note: First handheld: parents to a hand-grip socket rather than a surface socket.
